_source: Šumma izbu Tablet I (Standard Babylonian series of birth/teratomantic omens), Neo-/Late Babylonian exemplar MS 1808. Edited by A. R. George, Babylonian Divinatory Texts Chiefly in the Schøyen Collection (CUSAS 18), Bethesda: CDL Press, 2013, no. 35, pp. 259-261. English translation: George 2013.
_note: Tablet I of Šumma izbu concerns HUMAN births; the Ugaritic KTU 1.103 omens in sheep_birth_omens.json concern SHEEP/GOAT births. Both belong to the same Mesopotamian teratomancy (izbu) tradition. Line numbers refer to George's edition. '[...]' marks a break in the tablet.

gives birth to an animal.lion (l.5): that town will be taken, its king will be captured
gives birth to an animal.wolf (l.6): the people's minds will become deranged
gives birth to an animal.dog (l.7): the head of the household will die and that household will be scattered; the people's minds will become deranged; plague will devour
gives birth to an animal.pig (l.8): a woman will occupy the throne
gives birth to an animal.ox (l.9): there will be an all-powerful king in the land
gives birth to an animal.buffalo (l.10): there will be an all-powerful king in the land and the land will experience misery
gives birth to an animal.wild bull (l.11): the king will encounter no equal
gives birth to an animal.donkey (l.13): there will be an all-powerful king in the land
gives birth to an animal.sheep (l.14): the prince will encounter no equal
gives birth to an animal.cat (l.15): the prince will encounter no equal
gives birth to an animal.snake (l.16): the man's household will be flogged(?)

gives birth to an unusual form.terrapin's head (l.17): (it is) the portent of Ur; devastation will occur in the land

gives birth to multiple children.four or five boys (l.123): that people will be scattered; the dynasty will change
gives birth to multiple children.four or five girls (l.124): that land will become more populous; the man's household will expand
gives birth to multiple children.six (l.129): that land will become more populous; the king will conquer his foes
gives birth to multiple children.seven (l.130): rise of a rebel king; the people's minds will become deranged; people will sell their infant children for silver
gives birth to multiple children.seven or eight (l.131): a rebel king will arise and the land will become depopulated

unborn baby in the womb.grips the mother (l.2): the kor-measure will be in short supply; the man's household will be scattered
unborn baby in the womb.groans (l.3): the palace will requisition the man's house
unborn baby in the womb.calls out audibly (l.4): a mighty enemy will arise and destroy the land; the enemy will consume its finest goods
